markdownRemark.html:
So, recently I got my hands on an e-paper display. I'm talking about this one. Doesn't seem that impressive at first, but it's really fun to get your feet wet with hardware programming. The display gets mounted on the GPIO pins of a Raspberry Pi and can render texts and images, either in black or red.

But why though?

Because I wanted to do something else than web devevlopment / Frontend stuff for a change. :-)

And what does it do?

The display has 4 buttons on which I placed different functions, written in Python.

  • Key1: Clear display, to prevent any burn in / ghosting
  • Key2: Fetch todos from my Nextcloud server sorted by creation date
  • Key3: Coffee break! Renders a nice cup of coffee
  • Key4: Fetch current weather information from openweathermap (huge shoutout to Protostax and their tutorial, which really made the start easy for me!)

See the source code here: https://github.com/v-glb/ePaper-dashboard
